You finally found a page dedicated to flat faced dogs.

Pugs, French Bulldogs, English Bulldogs, Boston Terriers, Shih Tzus, Cavaliers. The squishy faced ones. The ones that snore on the sofa, snort at strangers, fall asleep mid sentence, and somehow take up the entire bed despite being roughly the size of a loaf of bread.

They are also the breeds that need the most informed owners. Their flat faces (the proper term is brachycephalic) mean smaller airways, sensitive skin folds, eyes that sit closer to the world than most, and a body that runs hot faster than other dogs. The same features that make them so loved are the features that make their daily care a little more specific.

Here is what we cover on this page. How to keep them cool in summer. How to spot breathing trouble before it becomes serious. What to feed them, how to walk them, and which everyday habits add real years to their life. UK based, vet informed, written for the owners who want to get it right.

Your dog did not choose the body it was born into. You did choose to give it a good life. That is the whole job.
